About Me

Where logic meets humanity

I grew up in Hanoi believing that the two most important things a person can do are think clearly and act with kindness. At HUS High School for Gifted Students — one of Vietnam's most competitive specialized schools — I've spent the last year pushing both of those instincts as far as they can go.

By day I'm solving algorithmic problems in the Informatics track, debugging late into the night, and preparing for the next coding competition. By weekend I'm boarding trains to flood-hit provinces, showing up at children's hospitals, and helping rebuild a school in the mountains of Nghệ An. These don't feel like separate parts of me — they feel like the same impulse, expressed differently.

I believe education is not just a personal achievement — it is a responsibility to give back to the community that shaped you.

— Lâm An Bình, personal essay

I'm not chasing a perfect transcript for its own sake. I'm building skills because problems worth solving are hard, and hard problems deserve sharp tools. I volunteer because the world outside a classroom is the only place those tools truly matter.

Academic Journey

Built for the long game

🏛️
HUS High School for Gifted Students

Ranked among Vietnam's most selective high schools, attached to VNU University of Science. Admitted to the Informatics specialized track (Class 10A2 Tin) — a cohort that blends rigorous mathematics, computer science theory, and competitive programming.

💻
Informatics Specialization

Core coursework spans algorithms & data structures, discrete mathematics, and computer systems. My interest leans toward competitive programming — approaching problems as puzzles where elegance matters as much as correctness.

🌐
Language Proficiency

English proficiency earned a 9.8/10 average across the academic year — currently preparing standardized qualifications. Participated in the Open Day at The Chinese University of Hong Kong, Shenzhen (March 2025).

⭐
Academic Honor

Awarded the Certificate of Excellence (Học sinh Xuất sắc) for the academic year 2024–2025 — the school's highest individual academic distinction, recognizing both performance and conduct.

Grade 10 · Full Year Results (2024–2025)

Achievements

A record built one challenge at a time

January 2026
ICPC Vietnam 2026 — Competitive Programming

Represented HUS High School for Gifted Students at ICPC Vietnam — one of Asia's most prestigious collegiate-level programming contests. Competed as one of the youngest participants, tackling multi-hour algorithmic challenges under pressure.

🏆 ICPC Vietnam
May 2025
HSGS Olympiad — Informatics Competition

Selected to participate in the school's official Informatics Olympiad (Olympic Chuyên KHTN — Môn Tin học), a rigorous two-day internal competition testing advanced algorithmic and problem-solving abilities.

🎯 HSGS Olympiad · May 10–11, 2025
Academic Year 2024–2025
Certificate of Excellence — Student of the Year

Awarded the school's highest academic honor — Học sinh Xuất sắc — for Grade 10. Recognized by principal Dr. Lê Công Lợi for exceptional academic performance and outstanding conduct throughout the full academic year.

🎖️ Certificate of Excellence
March 2025
CUHK Shenzhen Open Day — International Exposure

Attended the Open Day at The Chinese University of Hong Kong, Shenzhen — representing HSGS and gaining firsthand exposure to international university research culture and campus life.

🌏 CUHK · Shenzhen · March 20–21, 2025
2024–2025
Student 3 Tốt — University-Level Recognition

Received the Học sinh 3 Tốt (Three-Good Student) recognition at the university campus level of ĐHKHTN — awarded for academic excellence, social conduct, and active community contribution.

⭐ 3 Tốt Award · ĐHKHTN
2023–2024 (Grade 9)
Volunteer Recognition — Hà Đông Hospital (Grade 9)

While still in middle school (Grade 9, Archimedes Secondary), volunteered with the Biệt đội An sinh xã hội team at Hà Đông General Hospital — earning formal recognition for charitable contributions during Children's Day activities.

❤️ Community Service · Hà Đông Hospital
Projects & Research

Things I've built and explored

01
Algorithmic Problem Solving Portfolio

An ongoing collection of competitive programming solutions — spanning graph algorithms, dynamic programming, and combinatorics — built through preparation for ICPC and HSGS Olympiad.

C++ Algorithms Data Structures
02
HSGS Code Camp — Teaching Assistant

Served as a Supporter (Trợ giảng) at HSGS Code Camp 2025, mentoring junior students through hands-on programming exercises — bridging the gap between abstract theory and applied code.

Pedagogy Mentorship Leadership
03
HSGS Code Camp — Volunteer Organizer

Contributed as a volunteer organizer (Tình nguyện viên) for HSGS Code Camp 2025 (June 4–15), coordinating logistics and participant support under the VNU University of Science trade union.

Event Org. Coordination ĐHKHTN
04
Yên Bái Flood Relief — "Fly to Sky" Campaign

Co-organized relief distribution for flood-affected communities in Yên Bái province (September 14, 2024) — coordinating with the National Volunteer Centre and local government to deliver aid packages.

Humanitarian Logistics Community
05
Nghệ An School Construction — "Busy Bee" Volunteer

Participated in the Busy Bee Friends Volunteer Group (Nhóm Ong Chăm), contributing to the physical construction of Hạnh Dịch Primary and Secondary School in Quế Phong, Nghệ An.

Rural Education Construction NGO
06
Children's Hospital Volunteer — "Nâng Vàng Ong"

Volunteered with Club NVO's "Nâng Vàng Ong" program at Hà Đông General Hospital (May 28, 2025), supporting pediatric patients during International Children's Day celebrations.

Healthcare Child Welfare Social Impact
Activities & Leadership

Beyond the classroom

⚽
Football Team — ĐHKHTN
School Sports · 2024–2025

Active member of the HUS High School football team, representing the school in intramural and inter-school competitions. Certified participant through the ĐHKHTN sports department — building teamwork and resilience under pressure.

🖥️
HSGS Code Camp — Instructor & Organizer
Teaching · June 2025

Wore two hats at Code Camp 2025: as a teaching assistant guiding junior students through programming fundamentals, and as a logistics volunteer ensuring the two-week residential event ran smoothly for all participants.

🤝
National Volunteer Centre — Disaster Relief
Humanitarian · Sept 2024

Coordinated with national and provincial bodies (Đoàn huyện Văn Yên, LHTN Yên Bái) to deliver flood relief to Yên Bái following typhoon damage — planning logistics, packing goods, and conducting on-the-ground distribution.

🏗️
Busy Bee Friends — Rural School Builder
Education Access · 2024–2025

One of many students who rolled up their sleeves to help build a school in one of Vietnam's most remote mountain communities — working alongside professional builders in Quế Phong, Nghệ An, to give children access to a proper learning space.

Core Values

What I stand for

  • 🔬
    Intellectual Rigor

    Believing that half-understood ideas are dangerous — I push to understand things fully, from first principles, before applying them.

  • 🌱
    Empathy in Action

    Compassion without action is just sympathy. I've learned to show up — physically, practically, and consistently — for communities in need.

  • 🔗
    Perseverance

    The problems worth solving — in code and in life — rarely yield quickly. I've trained myself to stay calm when I'm stuck and trust that the solution exists.

  • 🌍
    Community Responsibility

    As someone who has received excellent education, I feel a genuine obligation to use it in service of people who haven't had the same access.

Interests & Hobbies

Life outside school

💡 Competitive Programming
⚽ Football
📚 History & Philosophy
🎯 Problem-Solving Puzzles
🏕️ Volunteer Expeditions

🧠 Mathematics

The more I volunteer, the better my thinking gets. Sitting with hard realities forces you to be honest about what you know and what you don't.

— Personal reflection, 2024

I want to build technology that reaches the people who need it most — not the people who can afford it most.

— Lâm An Bình · Future Goals Statement
Future Goals

A clear horizon

2027
University — Computer Science

Pursue a degree in Computer Science at a top-ranked domestic or international institution — with a particular interest in applied algorithms and human-computer interaction.

2028
Research in Accessible Technology

Explore graduate-level research at the intersection of software engineering and social impact — building tools for education, disaster response, or healthcare in underserved communities.

2030+
Build Something That Matters

Whether as a researcher, entrepreneur, or engineer — contribute meaningfully to a world where access to quality education and technology is not a privilege but a right.
